hero_063.earlyGame: In the early game, it is important for Yuhuan to quickly clear minions by utilizing the 100% increased damage to minions from her Passive in the Dissonant stance. Use Skill 1 to deal damage and slow enemies with notes, and hit them with the enhanced basic attack to reduce skill cooldowns and take control of the lane. If allies take damage, switch to the Harmonious stance to support them with healing, developing a stable laning phase as your basic early game strategy.
hero_063.midGame: In the mid game, positioning utilizing Skill 2 is required. Increase your movement speed while slowing enemies, aiming for the stun after 1.5 seconds to assist allies in securing kills. As small-scale teamfights increase, the flexibility to switch between AoE healing with the Harmonious stance and damage with the Dissonant stance according to the situation is crucial. Also, always be mindful of the CD reduction from Skill 1's enhanced basic attack, contributing to fights while maintaining a high skill rotation rate.
hero_063.lateGame: In the late game, the invincibility frame, wide-area healing, and damage of Skill 3 become highly powerful weapons. Your greatest role is to avoid the enemy's strong CC and burst damage with Skill 3 while significantly healing surrounding allies to maintain the frontline. Additionally, using the stun from Skill 2 to catch the enemy carry or protect allies is also important. Always stick with your allies, and use Passive switching and CD reduction from Skill 1 to lead your team to victory in all-out teamfights.
hero_063.teamfight: In teamfights, switching Passives according to the situation and the timing of Skill 3 will determine victory or defeat. When engaging, restrict enemy movement with Skill 2 and aim for the stun. As ally HP decreases, heal them with the Harmonious stance, and the moment strong enemy attacks or CC fly in, use Skill 3 to become untargetable, cleansing CC while healing allies. Continuously fire Skill 1 and keep reducing CDs with enhanced basic attacks to provide sustained support and disruption.
hero_063.commonMistakes: A common mistake is forgetting to switch Passive stances. Remaining in the Harmonious stance when you should be attacking, or staying in the Dissonant stance when allies are in a pinch, prevents you from utilizing her strengths. Also, wasting Skill 3 simply as a damage source means you cannot avoid fatal enemy attacks or CC, leading to being easily killed in teamfights. Missing out on the CD reduction benefit by not weaving in the enhanced basic attack after Skill 1 should also be avoided.
hero_063.strengths: Her greatest strength is the balance of damage and healing through Passive switching, as well as her strong self-defense and ally support capabilities with Skill 3. While Skill 3 is active, she becomes untargetable and cleanses CC, giving her very high survivability and the ability to make enemy skills whiff. Additionally, the CD reduction effect of Skill 1 ensures a high skill rotation rate, and the versatility of Skill 2 for chasing and escaping with its movement speed boost and stun is also a powerful advantage.
hero_063.weaknesses: While she can balance healing and damage, her instantaneous burst damage is modest, and she lacks the ability to quickly burst down enemies alone. Also, since there is a 1.5-second delay before Skill 2's stun activates, it is easily dodged by highly mobile enemies and is somewhat unreliable as a guaranteed CC. Her survivability while Skill 3 is on cooldown relies on the movement speed boost from Skill 2, so she has the weakness of being very fragile if approached by an enemy assassin without Skill 3 available.

hero_064.earlyGame: In the early game for Cang, utilizing bushes with his Passive is essential. Entering a bush makes his basic attack fire two consecutive shots and increases damage to monsters by 25%, allowing him to gain an advantage in jungle clearing and lane harassment. Place Skill 2 traps in bushes and enemy pathing to secure vision, guard against ganks, and apply pressure with continuous damage and slow from the wolf spirit. It is basic practice to save Skill 1, which allows terrain crossing, for escaping or chasing.
hero_064.midGame: In the mid game, it is important to utilize the strength of placing up to four Skill 2 traps to control vision around dragons and inside the enemy jungle. Since a wolf spirit is summoned to slow enemies who step on the trap, you can develop small-scale fights advantageously. Once you acquire the mounted archery ability from Skill 3, use your mobility to dodge enemy attacks while dealing continuous damage. Making full use of jumping from bushes and crossing terrain with Skill 1 to take enemies by surprise is required.
hero_064.lateGame: In the late game, the mounted archery capability from Skill 3 with significantly increased firepower is highly potent. Because you can perform basic attacks while moving, you can dodge enemy skills while consistently pumping out damage from a safe position. The knockback attack from the air with Skill 1 is extremely important as a self-defense measure to protect against enemy assassins. Deploy traps on the battlefield to secure vision and damage zones, and fully unleash your firepower while confusing the enemy with high mobility involving bush utilization from your Passive.
hero_064.teamfight: In teamfights, it is important to scatter Skill 2 traps across the battlefield beforehand to secure vision and keep enemies in check. After combat begins, activate Skill 3 and reliably whittle down the enemy frontline with basic attacks while moving. If an enemy threat approaches, jump into the air and cross terrain with Skill 1, or create distance with the knockback from the mid-air basic attack. It is ideal to maintain a safe position by fully utilizing bush jumps and continuously outputting damage alongside the wolf spirit.
hero_064.commonMistakes: Misjudging when to use Skill 1 leads to major mistakes. Jumping into the enemy formation in a defenseless state, or wasting it when it should be saved as a terrain-crossing escape skill, makes you a prime target for enemy assassins. Also, fighting standing still on flat ground without being mindful of the Passive's two consecutive shots or jumps utilizing bushes completely fails to draw out Cang's potential. Engaging in combat in areas without vision without placing Skill 2 traps should also be avoided.
hero_064.strengths: His greatest strength is the mounted archery ability from Skill 3, which allows basic attacks while moving, and his overwhelming mobility from his Passive and Skill 1. Through bush jumps, jumping into the air and crossing terrain with Skill 1, as well as the knockback effect, he can continuously deal one-sided damage without allowing enemies to approach. Furthermore, the vision control and slow application of his Skill 2 traps are excellent advantages for both map control and self-defense.
hero_064.weaknesses: While he excels in mobility and sustained firepower, he somewhat lacks instantaneous burst damage, so enemies may escape before their HP is fully depleted. Additionally, because much of his damage source relies on basic attacks and the wolf spirit, he is weak against enemies with items or skills that reduce attack speed. He is highly dependent on the player's mechanical skill; failing to properly execute mid-air movement with Skill 1, kiting with the mounted archery ability, and utilizing bushes will result in him being easily killed without demonstrating his true strengths.

hero_065.earlyGame: In the early game, Yang Jian's damage trades utilizing Skill 2's sweep are potent. Because the stun duration is doubled when hitting Skill 2 while your HP percentage is lower than the enemy's, a strategy of intentionally taking a little damage before counterattacking is effective. Basic attacks after a Skill 2 hit apply true damage, ensuring reliable damage output. Utilize Skill 1 for long-range poke, securing vision, and as a finishing blow when the enemy's HP is low.
hero_065.midGame: In the mid game, utilize the mobility and kill potential from Skill 1 to actively participate in ganks on other lanes and small-scale fights. Hitting minions or enemy heroes with Skill 1 and reactivating it to instantly close the distance is a powerful move. Since killing an enemy resets Skill 1's CD, aiming for consecutive kills is also possible. Because his Passive grants increased resistance and super armor by sustaining combat, he plays the role of disrupting the enemy formation while fighting tenaciously on the frontline.
hero_065.lateGame: In the late game, he functions as a teamfight initiator or a diver targeting the enemy backline. Reliably stick to the enemy carry with Skill 1, and instantly apply pressure with Skill 2's AoE stun and true damage. Even in the late game where taken damage increases, he can exhibit high survivability through HP recovery from Skill 3's lasers, and the super armor and max HP increase from his Passive. Hitting multiple targets with Skill 3 right in the middle of the enemy formation and fighting persistently while healing is a crucial late-game strategy.
hero_065.teamfight: In teamfights, diving into the center of the enemy is required to maximize the increased resistance and super armor from his Passive. The ideal combo is to mark the enemy backline with Skill 1 to dash in, then catch multiple targets in Skill 2 to stun them. When HP gets low, fire Skill 3's lasers in the direction where enemies are clustered to deal damage while significantly healing HP to survive. Combined with the pursuit and healing from the Heavenly Eye laser of his Passive, he functions as a formidable wall.
hero_065.commonMistakes: A common mistake is missing the first phase of Skill 1, or blindly activating the second phase and ending up isolated deep in the enemy formation. Since Skill 1's CD resets when used for a finishing blow, abruptly using the second phase on an enemy with ample HP remaining prevents you from fully unleashing your firepower. Also, engaging in combat with full HP without being mindful of Skill 2's doubled stun effect means you cannot capitalize on your strength of prolonged CC, potentially resulting in unfavorable trades.
hero_065.strengths: His strengths are his high 1v1 capability due to Skill 2's true damage and conditional long stun, and his excellent self-healing abilities from Skill 3 and his Passive's Heavenly Eye. The resistance and super armor granted by his Passive make him highly resistant to CC and grant very high survivability in teamfights, which is highly appealing. With Skill 1's missing HP percentage damage and CD reset upon kill, he also harbors high carry potential to chain-kill enemies one after another in chaotic brawls.
hero_065.weaknesses: As for weaknesses, since his primary dash, Skill 1, is a directional skillshot, missing it drastically reduces his mobility and deprives him of a means to approach enemies. Also, because his healing ability relies on Skill 3's hit rate and number of targets, having it dodged by highly mobile enemies prevents him from tanking as expected. His survivability significantly drops if enemies buy anti-heal items, and managing the risk of intentionally lowering HP to aim for Skill 2's doubled stun is also a notable weakness.

hero_066.earlyGame: In the early game, Nuwa's fundamental approach is to utilize the expanded vision and range from her Passive to clear minions and poke enemies from a safe distance. Take control of the lane with the knockback and matrix deployment from Skill 1's energy blast. Placing Skill 2's matrix spaces in positions that restrict enemy movement, and hitting them with Skill 1 to trigger an explosion combo, is an effective way to rack up damage. Since her firepower is modest early on, avoid forced fights and safely level up.
hero_066.midGame: From the mid game onwards, her vision and range expand further as she levels up, making it easier to grasp the entire map. By acquiring Skill 3's long-distance warp, sudden strikes on other lanes and covering allies become instantly possible. Warping to areas where teamfights are likely to erupt to deploy a shield, cutting off enemy escape routes with Skill 2's matrices, and disrupting formations with Skill 1 is a powerful sequence. Aim for matrix combination explosions to scatter AoE damage and CC, taking control of the battle situation.
hero_066.lateGame: In the late game, Nuwa's range and vision reach their maximum, exhibiting overwhelming poke capability and board control. Simply placing Skill 2 from outside the enemy's vision and detonating it with Skill 1 can inflict fatal damage to the enemy backline. Skill 2's obstacles prevent enemies from approaching, boasting immense power in defensive battles and tower sieges. Skill 3's warp is useful for moving into enemy blind spots or securing escape routes. Act as a commander maneuvering the entire battlefield from the safety of the rear.
hero_066.teamfight: Nuwa's role in teamfights is continuous damage output from long range and terrain control. Before combat starts, place multiple Skill 2 matrices to block enemy advance routes and divide the battlefield. Fire Skill 1 in sync with your allies' CC to trigger chain explosions of the matrices, dealing massive damage and knockbacks. If an enemy assassin approaches, knock them away with Skill 1, and if in danger, it is vital to warp to a safe location with Skill 3 to maintain distance.
hero_066.commonMistakes: A common mistake for Nuwa is misjudging the warp destination of Skill 3, diving into the middle of the enemy group and becoming isolated. Although she gains a shield after warping, her inherent durability is low, making her easy to kill. Placing Skill 2 matrices in the path of allies, hindering their pursuit or escape, is also a fatal positioning error. Fighting defenselessly close to the enemy's attack range without utilizing the long range of her Passive is another typical mistake that must be avoided.
hero_066.strengths: Her greatest strengths are her overwhelming vision and long range from her Passive, and her battlefield control capabilities through Skill 2's obstacle creation. She can unilaterally deal damage from a safe distance and break enemy formations. The AoE firepower from the chain explosions of Skill 2 and Skill 1 is tremendous, showing unparalleled strength in defensive battles. Furthermore, she possesses outstanding mobility and cover capability with Skill 3's long-distance warp, giving her the major advantage of being able to instantly intervene anywhere on the map.
hero_066.weaknesses: While she excels in long range and control capabilities, she is extremely weak in close-quarters combat, making it difficult to cope if highly mobile enemy assassins slip into her personal space. Missing Skill 1's knockback leaves her with almost no means of self-defense. Also, due to the time lag from skill activation to impact, she is easily dodged by quick enemies, demanding high prediction accuracy to output damage consistently. Falling into a lack of firepower by mismanaging Skill 2's placement limit and cooldowns is also a weakness.

hero_067.earlyGame: In the early game, Nezha seizes lane control by leveraging his strength in close combat utilizing Skill 1. Skill 1 deals damage to the surroundings while applying true damage and anti-heal from Scorching Flame, granting a huge advantage in damage trades. Hitting skills grants a true damage shield and movement speed boost from his Passive, so the basic approach is to maneuver behind the enemy with Skill 2, adding more shields, and overwhelmingly pressuring the enemy through persistent fighting.
hero_067.midGame: In the mid game, acquiring Skill 3's long-range dash allows him to intervene in combat from anywhere on the map. When fights break out in other lanes or encounters happen in the jungle, instantly target the enemy carry or an isolated foe and dive in. Upon landing, he gains damage reduction and increased resistance, allowing him to endure to some extent even in the center of the enemy formation. Scatter Scorching Flame on multiple enemies with Skill 1, applying anti-heal while securing a true damage shield to support the frontline.
hero_067.lateGame: In the late game, his primary role is to reliably eliminate the enemy's key targets or force them out of the battlefield. Do not miss the timing when teamfights start or the moment the enemy carry becomes isolated, and dive in accurately with Skill 3. Since the Passive shield limit increases with bonus HP, continuously hitting skills on multiple targets unlocks incredible durability. Disrupt the enemy formation and gradually whittle down the entire enemy team with the sustained damage of Scorching Flame.
hero_067.teamfight: Nezha's most crucial mission in teamfights is reliably diving the enemy backline with Skill 3. At the start of combat, dive toward the enemy's main damage source and disrupt their formation with the knockback. After landing, spam Skill 1 to apply Scorching Flame to as many enemies as possible, continually putting up Passive shields. Utilize Skill 2 to dodge enemy attacks while sticking closely to them, acting as a decoy to draw the enemy's attention and allow your allies to act freely.
hero_067.commonMistakes: The biggest mistake is diving alone with Skill 3 at a timing or location where allies cannot follow up, resulting in isolation and a pointless death. Since the dash cannot be canceled mid-flight, misjudging the situation leaves you stranded deep inside enemy lines. Moreover, failing to maintain the Passive shield and Skill 1's Scorching Flame drastically drops his durability and firepower, making whiffing skills fatal. Forgetting the mechanic that hitting minions drastically reduces shield generation is also dangerous.
hero_067.strengths: Nezha's strengths are his reliable long-range dive capability with his targeted Skill 3, and his overwhelming durability in chaotic brawls thanks to the true damage shield from his Passive. Being able to directly target the enemy backline from anywhere constantly pressures the enemy carry. Also, Skill 1's Scorching Flame applies true damage and anti-heal, making him extremely strong against heroes with high healing capabilities; his self-sufficient combat power, where continuously dealing damage recovers his shield, is highly appealing.
hero_067.weaknesses: As a weakness, since Skill 3's dash has no point of return once activated, failing to kill the enemy or lacking ally backup ensures you lose your escape route. Shield generation requires hitting skills on enemy heroes as a prerequisite, so having skills dodged by highly mobile enemies makes his durability brittle. His instantaneous burst damage is low, relying heavily on sustained damage, making it hard to counter sudden burst firepower that shatters his shield and kills him instantly.

hero_068.earlyGame: In the early game, Ganjiang & Moye focus on safe lane clearing and poking using the range of their flying swords from Skill 2 and Skill 3. Since the Passive fires two flying swords simultaneously, you can aim for massive damage by accurately hitting minions or enemy heroes with the intersection of their trajectories. From the early game, utilize the bonus damage after applying sword energy to whittle down enemy HP and apply pressure. If approached, it is crucial to use Skill 1 to knock back the enemy while retreating yourself to maintain a safe distance.
hero_068.midGame: In the mid game, rotating to other lanes and providing backline support in small-scale fights become your main roles. Accurately hitting the intersection of the flying swords begins to show the potential to shave off more than half the HP of squishy enemy heroes. Moreover, since Skill 1's Passive effect permanently increases magical attack with each kill or assist, it is important to actively participate in fights to build stacks. Increase your firepower with combos incorporating Skill 1's magical defense reduction effect.
hero_068.lateGame: In the late game, Ganjiang & Moye's firepower reaches its zenith, possessing overwhelming poke damage capable of leaving the enemy backline near death or instantly killing them just by landing flying swords. With the bonus damage from the Passive and the stack effect from Skill 1, you can leverage your range advantage to collapse the enemy formation. Always secure a safe position behind your allies and relentlessly fire Skill 2 and Skill 3. Against enemy ambushes, executing self-defense accurately with Skill 1 is the top priority.
hero_068.teamfight: In teamfights, never step out to the frontline; instead, function as an artillery piece continuously firing Skill 2 and Skill 3 from the furthest safe position. The ideal is to smash the intersection of the flying swords into the center of the enemy, synchronized with your allies' CC. Apply sword energy with the first flying sword, and trigger bonus damage with the subsequent ones to annihilate enemies. If an enemy assassin approaches, calmly activate Skill 1 to create distance with the knockback and retreat, then turn the tables by landing flying swords on the target with lowered magical defense.
hero_068.commonMistakes: The biggest mistake is missing the intersection of the uniquely trajected flying swords. Failing to hit the intersection more than halves the damage, wasting the Passive's sword energy and causing a lack of firepower. Furthermore, wasting Skill 1, your sole means of self-defense, for movement or trivial damage leaves you with no escape when approached by an enemy assassin, leading to easy deaths. Carelessly stepping too far forward without being mindful of safe positioning is also a fatal mistake to avoid.
hero_068.strengths: Ganjiang & Moye's strength is their overwhelming instantaneous burst magical damage unleashed from an ultra-long range. Their poke capability to instantly rob enemy HP from a position immune to counterattack by hitting the flying sword intersection is top-tier among all heroes. Due to the infinite stacking growth of magical attack from Skill 1's Passive, their firepower becomes increasingly uncontrollable the later the game goes. Additionally, the simultaneous knockback and retreat from Skill 1 serves as an exceptionally excellent self-defense mechanism for a Mage.
hero_068.weaknesses: As a weakness, because the trajectories of the flying swords form a peculiar shape of intersecting curves, aiming difficulty is extremely high, and firepower heavily depends on the player's mechanical skill. Landing skills on fast-moving enemies is difficult. Moreover, they are utterly defenseless in close quarters while Skill 1 is on cooldown, and their low mobility means survival is hopeless once an enemy gets inside their guard. Without sustained damage sources, continually having skills dodged results in a remarkably low contribution in teamfights.

hero_069.earlyGame: Athena's early game strength lies in her aggressive playstyle utilizing her high mobility and shields. Close the gap with the jump and enhanced basic attack charge from Skill 2, and turn damage trades in your favor with the shield deployment and knockback of Skill 1. Since Skill 2's CD resets when Skill 1 hits, you can unleash charges continuously. During jungle clears, the bonus damage of her enhanced basic attacks against non-heroes shines, enabling fast farming and proactive ganks.
hero_069.midGame: In the mid game, she roams the battlefield utilizing her excellent pursuit capability and CC resistance in small-scale fights. By chaining the shields of Skill 1 or Skill 3 after approaching with Skill 2, she sticks to her targets without being hindered by enemies, reliably executing them with her missing HP percentage damage. Even if you happen to be killed, moving around in the True God state of her Passive allows you to secure vision for allies and grant them a movement speed buff; thus, you should prepare to resurrect in an optimal position while supporting your team even after death.
hero_069.lateGame: In the late game, as enemy firepower increases, managing the shield of Skill 3 becomes highly important. In teamfights, dive into the frontline, bait out powerful enemy skills, and absorb them with Skill 3's shield; meanwhile, utilize the increased counterattack damage and shortened detonation time from taking damage to inflict massive AoE damage. Flawlessly cycling through the CD reset combos triggered by hitting Skill 2 and other skills is required to combine high durability and mobility while wreaking havoc on the enemy formation.
hero_069.teamfight: In teamfights, Athena functions as a Fighter who dives into the enemy lines to disrupt their formation. Charge at the enemy carry with Skill 2, instantly deploy Skill 3 to absorb the enemy's focused fire and CC with the shield. If the counterattack triggers without the shield breaking, massive damage is dealt and Skill 2's CD is reset, allowing for further pursuit or disengagement. Even after death, fly around your allies in the True God state and re-intervene in combat with the knockup upon resurrection.
hero_069.commonMistakes: Lack of precision in skill combos is the most common mistake. Failing to hit enemies with Skill 1 or Skill 3 means the CD for your primary Skill 2 won't reset, causing a drastic drop in mobility and firepower and leaving you isolated in enemy territory. Also, while Skill 3 is powerful, it renders you immobile; using it in situations where enemies can easily escape or where you take damage exceeding the shield and die before the counterattack is dangerous. Setting your resurrection position right in the middle of the enemy formation and getting spawn-killed is also a classic mistake.
hero_069.strengths: Her greatest strengths are her overwhelming mobility through the continuous use of Skill 2, and her high anti-control performance utilizing the shields and CC immunity from Skills 1 and 3. She possesses the firepower to ignore enemy CC, stick to carries, and even melt Tanks with her percentage damage. Furthermore, her unique Passive, which allows her to be controlled and provide vision even after death, mitigates the penalty of dying; this tactical flexibility, letting her resurrect in any location and instantly return to combat with a knockup, is a huge advantage.
hero_069.weaknesses: As for weaknesses, her mana consumption is extremely intense, and without proper mana management, she quickly runs out of gas. Since her durability relies on shields, she is highly vulnerable to firepower capable of breaking her shields or enemies with true damage. Because her design assumes skill CD resets, whiffing combos or being dodged by enemy mobility leaves her helpless. High mechanical difficulty, where her performance heavily hinges on the player's mechanics and judgment, is also a weakness.

hero_070.earlyGame: In the early game, Cai Yan's main role is to stay close to the allied Marksman and support a stable laning phase. When taking damage from enemies, her Passive effect increases her movement speed and triggers auto-healing, so moving slightly forward to bait enemy attacks using this effect is also valid. The ricocheting sound wave from Skill 2 is a powerful poking tool that can travel through minions to stun enemy heroes, so aim to cast it precisely when minion waves meet to create favorable trades.
hero_070.midGame: In small-scale teamfights from the mid game onwards, Cai Yan's healing abilities and CC truly shine. Use Skill 1 when ally HP drops, leveraging the movement speed buff to provide AoE healing for the entire team. Fire Skill 2 into areas where enemies are clustered, continuously stunning multiple targets to disrupt their coordination. If an enemy assassin dives your backline, it is crucial to immediately activate Skill 3 to vigorously heal the target's HP while raising their defense, preventing death beforehand.
hero_070.lateGame: In the late game, enemy firepower becomes overwhelming, but Cai Yan's healing output also skyrockets with items. As the linchpin of teamfights, positioning to ensure you are never the first to fall is the highest priority. Stick constantly to the allied carry, and reliably protect them from enemy burst damage with the massive single-target heal and defense buff from Skill 3. Endlessly cycling through Skill 1's AoE heal and Skill 2's multi-stuns to drag enemies into a battle of attrition that breaks their spirit is the winning formula for the late game.
hero_070.teamfight: In teamfights, position yourself in the backline and pay close attention to managing the HP of your entire team. As a signal to engage, fire Skill 2 into the enemy lines to stop their movement with chain stuns. The moment an enemy AoE attack hits your allies, use Skill 1 to heal the entire group, and do not hesitate to use Skill 3 on an ally who is focused and whose HP enters dangerous waters, raising their defense to save them. Utilize the movement speed buff from your Passive to escape enemy targeting, continuously maintaining the frontline as a walking fountain.
hero_070.commonMistakes: A common mistake is hoarding healing skills until an ally's HP is critically low, resulting in the heal not landing in time and the ally dying. Also, despite her own durability not being very high, forcibly approaching an ally deep in enemy lines to heal them, only to get caught in the crossfire and killed, is a classic blunder. Using Skill 2 when an enemy is isolated, failing to fully utilize her greatest strength of chain stuns via ricochets, is also a playstyle to avoid.
hero_070.strengths: Her greatest strength is her overwhelming healing capability that pulls allies back from the brink of death. Through Skill 1's AoE heal and Skill 3's massive single-target heal combined with a defense buff, she drastically enhances her team's sustained combat ability. Furthermore, Skill 2's sound wave becomes more effective the denser the enemies are, ricocheting between minions and enemy heroes to apply multiple stuns, yielding extremely high disruption potential in teamfights. She also possesses self-healing and a movement speed buff from her Passive, successfully balancing survivability and support capabilities at a high level.
hero_070.weaknesses: While she is specialized in healing, she entirely lacks the ability to output damage herself, rendering her helpless if her allies fail to deal damage. Her greatest natural enemies are items and skills with anti-heal effects; once built, her healing output drops drastically, halving her effectiveness. Additionally, the heavy mana consumption when casting skills makes mana management difficult, marking another weakness. Being caught by an enemy with strong CC exposes her fragility, as she can be bursted down instantly before having a chance to use healing skills, so constant vigilance over her positioning is necessary.

hero_071.earlyGame: In the early game, Donghuang boasts some of the strongest close-quarters combat prowess among all heroes. Pre-generating 3 dark orbs with Skill 1 and aggressively invading the enemy jungle to apply pressure is a highly effective move. By staying close to enemies and hitting them with the orbs, he deals max HP percentage damage while restoring his own HP, exhibiting overwhelming durability even when surrounded by multiple foes as long as the orbs hit. Focus entirely on wreaking havoc in the enemy jungle during the early game to build an advantage for your allies.
hero_071.midGame: In the mid game, his greatest weapon is Skill 3, acquired at level 4. Target troublesome enemy assassins or fed carries, forcibly approaching and suppressing them. Since damage taken during Suppression is shared between both parties, tactics such as having allies beat down the enemy alongside you, or tower diving to share the tower's damage, are lethal. When approaching, release the orbs with Skill 2 to boost your movement speed, locking the enemy down with the stun on the 3rd hit or the heavy slow on the 2nd hit before jumping in as the basic combo.
hero_071.lateGame: In the late game, the firepower of both teams is extremely high, so where Donghuang uses Skill 3 directly impacts victory or defeat. Carelessly suppressing the enemy Tank will result in being focus-fired by the enemy carry and dying for nothing, so you must reliably capture the main enemy carry, even if it requires utilizing Flash. By building abundant HP and defense, operate on the frontline as a figure of fear, prepared to absorb enemy attacks and drag them down with you.
hero_071.teamfight: His role in teamfights is to completely shut down the enemy carry with the absolute Suppression of Skill 3. Before combat, maintain 3 orbs and take on enemy poke as the frontline. At the timing of engagement, cast Skill 2 to disrupt formations with a wide-area slow and stun, charging the enemy backline by utilizing the movement speed boost. If you activate Skill 3 while stuck to the target, you will incapacitate them for 2 seconds while reliably securing the kill through shared damage. Utilize the healing from your orbs to survive the chaotic brawl.
hero_071.commonMistakes: A fatal mistake is using Skill 3 on a tough enemy Tank or Support. Due to the nature of damage sharing, suppressing an enemy with high HP will fail to finish them off, and conversely, you will receive focused fire and be killed. Furthermore, you must be cautious because immediately after firing Skill 2, your orbs are gone, temporarily depriving you of the healing and damage source from your Passive and making you very fragile. Charging into enemy lines without orbs or initiating a Suppression in an isolated position where allies cannot follow up are also typical blunders.
hero_071.strengths: His greatest strength is the guaranteed single-target kill capability via Skill 3's Suppression, which cannot even be cleansed (CC removal). No matter how fed the opponent is, as long as you catch them, you can forcefully drag them down with shared damage, meaning you always hold the potential for a comeback. In addition, the percentage damage and self-healing from the dark orbs of his Passive grant him astonishing toughness in early close combat and brawls, and his overwhelming invade capability to unilaterally disrupt the enemy jungle is an exceptionally excellent trait.
hero_071.weaknesses: As for weaknesses, he is completely helpless against long-range poke compositions or highly mobile enemies who refuse to enter the range of his orbs. Because he cannot heal unless he continuously lands orbs at close range, he goes down easily when whittled down from afar. Also, since he himself cannot move during Skill 3's Suppression, using it in a situation surrounded solely by enemies carries the risk of a one-sided demise. The activation of Skill 2 has a multi-second delay, making it easy to dodge if not fired predictively, which, coupled with his low mobility, is a bottleneck.

hero_072.earlyGame: In the early game, Guiguzi's aggressive ganks and harassment combining his Passive and skills are potent. Break line of sight with the enemy to prep the enhanced basic attack from your Passive, and use Skill 1 to enter camouflage and quickly close the gap. After touching the enemy to reduce their defense, inflict a slow and damage with the enhanced basic attack, and aim for the kill in tandem with your ally. Once you learn Skill 2 at Level 2, the pull and stun are added, allowing you to exert an overwhelming presence in small-scale fights early on and dictate the pace of the game.
hero_072.midGame: From the mid game onwards, leverage Skill 3 acquired at Level 4 to become the initiator for surprise attacks involving the entire team. In jungle encounters or battles around objectives like dragons, camouflage your allies with Skill 3 and burst into the heart of the enemy team. Lower enemy defenses with Skill 1, and ideally catch multiple enemies within Skill 2's range to pull and stun them. Because you can also grant allies a powerful shield, roam as the commander who favorably develops mid-game teamfights.
hero_072.lateGame: In the late game, precision in ambushes to instantaneously decimate the enemy is demanded. The team-wide camouflage and vision granted by Guiguzi's Skill 3 is the ultimate initiation tool capable of forcing a teamfight without giving the enemy a moment to react. Sync up with allied heroes capable of burst damage, and accurately catch the enemy carry with Skill 2. By applying defense reduction debuffs, it is possible to create scenarios where your allies instantly erase even the enemy's frontline Tanks.
hero_072.teamfight: His role in teamfights is to execute a flawless engage that shatters the enemy formation. Approach in stealth alongside allies with Skill 3, gaining vision of the nearest enemy hero to pinpoint their location. Utilize the movement speed buff from Skill 1 to slip into the center of the enemy group, touching every enemy to lower their defense. Time Skill 2's explosion perfectly to pull multiple targets to one spot and stun them. If this chain of combos lands, the outcome of the teamfight is practically decided.
hero_072.commonMistakes: A common mistake is diving the enemy lines alone without syncing the timing with allies. Guiguzi himself has low durability; even if Skill 2's pull succeeds, without allied follow-up, he will take concentrated fire and die instantly. Additionally, because there is a 2-second delay before Skill 2 triggers, mistiming it and letting enemies escape, or having the camouflage drop before pulling and exposing your defenseless self, are classic errors. Consuming the enhanced basic attack from the Passive on minions is also to be avoided.
hero_072.strengths: His greatest strength is the overwhelming initiation capability through granting the whole team camouflage with Skill 3 and the pull-stun from Skill 2. He can force a teamfight without giving the enemy time to react, harboring the potential to flip the tide of battle in an instant. Furthermore, the percentage reduction debuff on enemy physical and magical defense from Skill 1 is highly potent, boosting allied firepower. He is the pinnacle of aggressive Supports, able to constantly exert pressure across the entire map with high mobility.
hero_072.weaknesses: As a weakness, his defensive stats are low, relying on Skill 2's shield; thus, if an engage fails, he is built with an extremely high risk of being deleted instantly before he can run. Moreover, coordination with allies is an absolute prerequisite, so in solo play or uncommunicative teams, his strengths cannot be leveraged at all. Whiffing his skill damage and CC leaves him with nothing else to do, diminishing his presence. The fact that his strengths are easily neutralized if the enemy has heroes with wide-area CC or vision-granting skills is also a vulnerability.
